PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar May-24-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Arthur Silverman in propria persona (Petitioner) via CourtCall

The Court made inquiries regarding Mr. Silverman's knowledge of Probate procedures, and emphasized that probate administration are bound by rules that need to be followed properly and efficiently which may require assistance of an attorney. Mr. Silverman stated that he is located at Mammoth Lake, can appear in person for future hearing dates if necessary, has consulted with local probate attorney, that this is a simple case, there is only one heir, there is no real property, and the assets are de minimis. Mr. Silverman also stated that the decedent's brother (unaware of probate) cleaned out the decedent's apartment in San Francisco after death, disposed of personal property, they have made an agreement with the bank to release funds from a trust, and the bank required them to go through probate. The Court noted that he has issue appointing without an attorney when already mistakes have been made. In addition, Mr. Silverman stated that he is aware of next steps, i.e., accounting, inventory, working with examiner, filing a petition for final distribution, knows he cannot distributed anything until final distribution has been approved, and has confirmed with Probate Examiner that his proposed order was received yesterday. The Court granted The Petition for Letters of Administration, etc. (subject to review of the order). Status on filing of Inventory and Appraisal is set for September 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of Petition for Final Distribution is set for November 18, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
